milestone: Host Here didn't refresh Working View's stale hosting banner
slice: Found live authoring a Scene-hosted background-colour Experience: the Inspector's own 'Host Here' button (the multi-Scene picker) correctly wrote the new attachment -- Used In, the Scene's real background colour, everything underneath was already correct the instant the click handler ran -- but its own click handler only re-rendered the Context Panel, never Working View's Experience Studio banner (_renderExperienceStudio/_experienceHostingStatus), so that banner kept showing its pre-hosting "Not yet hosted anywhere" text right alongside an Inspector correctly reading "Used In: <Scene>" / "Already hosted -- nothing more to host here", a real, reproducible internal inconsistency. Switched to _redrawSceneCanvasesForExperience(exp), the helper already built for exactly this, matching the two sibling attach call sites (Create & Host, Reuse Existing) that already used the equivalent _renderWorkspace(). Verified via Playwright: the banner now correctly clears the instant the target Scene is the one currently open, and separately confirmed the underlying colour-sync mechanism (_syncUniversalContent -> setSceneBackground) was already correct all along -- the reported "not showing in Runtime Preview" was Runtime Preview showing a different, currently-open Scene than the one the Experience was hosted into, now clearly disclosed by the fixed banner's own wording ("Hosted in <Scene> -- open that Scene to see it in Runtime Preview") instead of the misleading "Not yet hosted anywhere". goldenBuild.js 30/30 unchanged.
